title: failed to build docker with operation not permitted
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

failed to build docker with operation not permitted with following command:
 podman build -t test .


STEP 15: RUN bin/jboss-cli.sh --file=cli/0-K8sCluster.cli -Dserver.config=standalone-full-ha.xml
error running container: error creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c bin/jboss-cli.sh --file=cli/test.cli ]: operation not permitted
Error: error building at STEP "RUN bin/jboss-cli.sh --file=cli/test.cli": error while running runtime: exit status 1
root@98ac70b9b05e:/# uname -a
Linux 98ac70b9b05e 3.10.0-1062.12.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Dec 12 06:44:49 EST 2019 x86_64 GNU/Linux
root@98ac70b9b05e:/# podman version
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
root@98ac70b9b05e:/#

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.podman build -t test .

2.

3.

**Describe the results you expected:**

comments.body: @stanltam can you share the Dockerfile that you used?

Hi Tom,
 Here you are.

FROM jboss/wildfly
USER 0
COPY build/cli/test.cli $JBOSS_HOME/cli/test.cli
WORKDIR $JBOSS_HOME
RUN bin/jboss-cli.sh --file=cli/test.cli  -Dserver.config=standalone-full-ha.xml
Are you running as root or rootless?
I am using root to run the container

First off could you see if SELinux is blocking some operation?  Does this work in permissive mode?
If it still breaks could you try
podman build --cap-add all ...
And see if it succeeds.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing due to inactivity. Feel free to reopen at any time.

Hi,

I am facing the same issue.

I am using AWX runnig in docker cotainer and I want to build a GO app.


/etc/containers/containers.conf : 

> 
> [containers]
> seccomp_profile = "/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json"
> [network]
> [engine]
> cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
> no_pivot_root = false
> [engine.runtimes]
> 


My docker file is : 

> FROM golang:latest
> LABEL maintainer="MBG <author@gmail.com>"
> WORKDIR /gogoogle
> COPY go.mod go.sum ./
> RUN go mod download
> RUN go get github.com/pilu/fresh
> COPY . .
> EXPOSE 80
> CMD ["make", "run-dev"]
> 

I have this error : 

> 
> .....
> --> Using cache e2a0b85c4d97818293fe74ff78a7fe818ef963bef43c0e93c48a3d9fdd54fedc
> STEP 3: WORKDIR /gogoogle
> --> Using cache b41c00e9a96de80cbebe3b0d49d3f265ce454716136dfb4985be1c02127ab5cd
> STEP 4: COPY go.mod go.sum ./
> --> Using cache 43091ac6413c081a4f916bb2b1e9dd7a73f9b12afe06af9e2ea0536fe0a569dc
> STEP 5: RUN go mod download
> error running container: error creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c go mod download]: operation not permitted
> Error: error building at STEP "RUN go mod download": error while running runtime: exit status 1

Any help will be welcomed.
Can you open a new issue against github.com/containers/buildah and include all the information the issue template there requests?
